description_long: This object is a boxy, window-mounted air conditioner unit with a rectangular main body and two accordion-style side panels designed to fit into a window frame. The front face features a prominent grille with horizontal and vertical slats for air output, as well as a control panel with two rotary dials. The sides of the unit display vented panels, one of which shows signs of use or weathering. The rear of the unit is covered by a dense mesh grille, likely for air intake and heat dissipation. The exterior is primarily a light gray or off-white color, with some darker accents on the grille and control panel. The overall construction appears robust, with visible screws and seams indicating a metal and plastic build.
description: A rectangular, window-mounted air conditioner with a front grille, control dials, and accordion side panels in light gray and dark accents.
description_view_0: The rear view shows a dense mesh grille covering the back, flanked by the extended accordion side panels.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals a vented panel with visible weathering and the depth of the main unit, as well as the side panel's thickness.
description_view_2: The front view displays the main air output grille, two rotary control dials, and the accordion side panels fully extended.
description_view_3: The right side view shows another vented panel, similar to the left, and the side profile of the control panel and grille.
attribute_grille_pattern: The front grille features horizontal slats on the upper section and a grid of vertical and horizontal slats on the lower section, both with regular spacing for airflow.
attribute_control_panel_location: bottom left of the front face
attribute_exhaust_vent_design: The exhaust vent is located on the rear face, covered by a dense mesh grille, with additional louvered vents on the side panels for heat dissipation.
